title: Fiberglass Laminators and Fabricators
description: Laminate layers of fiberglass on molds to form boat decks and hulls, bodies for golf carts, automobiles, or other products.

tasks.text: Release air bubbles and smooth seams, using rollers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Spray chopped fiberglass, resins, and catalysts onto prepared molds or dies using pneumatic spray guns with chopper attachments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix catalysts into resins, and saturate cloth and mats with mixtures, using brushes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check completed products for conformance to specifications and for defects by measuring with rulers or micrometers, by checking them visually, or by tapping them to detect bubbles or dead spots.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pat or press layers of saturated mat or cloth into place on molds, using brushes or hands, and smooth out wrinkles and air bubbles with hands or squeegees.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select precut fiberglass mats, cloth, and wood-bracing materials as required by projects being assembled.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bond wood reinforcing strips to decks and cabin structures of watercraft, using resin-saturated fiberglass.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trim excess materials from molds, using hand shears or trimming knives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply layers of plastic resin to mold surfaces prior to placement of fiberglass mats, repeating layers until products have the desired thicknesses and plastics have jelled.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect, clean, and assemble molds before beginning work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Cure materials by letting them set at room temperature, placing them under heat lamps, or baking them in ovens.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply lacquers and waxes to mold surfaces to facilitate assembly and removal of laminated parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair or modify damaged or defective glass-fiber parts, checking thicknesses, densities, and contours to ensure a close fit after repair.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mask off mold areas not to be laminated, using cellophane, wax paper, masking tape, or special sprays containing mold-release substances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check all dies, templates, and cutout patterns to be used in the manufacturing process to ensure that they conform to dimensional data, photographs, blueprints, samples, or customer specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trim cured materials by sawing them with diamond-impregnated cutoff wheels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
